description:
A successful negotiation outcome should carry a CommitPolicy variant chosen by the participant, so plugins can suppress, batch, or author the commit that records their result. Closes a story from the original design discussion: "ticket state should have commits / shouldn't have commits in updates" — different plugins want different behavior, and today the core hardcodes one-commit-per-op with a fixed message.

## Why this lives at the negotiation outcome

The participant primitive (bl-eae4) returns an Outcome on success. That is the right seam: every code path that applies a participant's result already routes through it. Bolting commit-policy onto every individual call site (push, sync, future events) would re-introduce the parallel-subsystem problem bl-b7fb is trying to eliminate.

## Variants (settled by bl-26de SPEC)

- Commit { message: Option<String> } — write a commit, optionally with a participant-supplied message. Default if absent.
- Batch { tag: String } — accumulate within the current event-dispatch and commit once at the end with a synthesized message referencing every batched participant. Multiple participants returning Batch with the same tag coalesce.
- Suppress — apply state to the working tree but do not commit. The next participant or operator commits. Disallowed for required participants (would break the bl-2148 fail-hard contract: a required outcome must be durable).

## Safety bounds

- Suppress is rejected at apply-time for required participants; the negotiation primitive errors with a clear message.
- Batch coalesces only within one lifecycle event invocation; never across CLI commands.
- A participant cannot suppress another participant's commit; policy is per-outcome, not global.
- Commit messages from plugins are wrapped with a "plugin: {name}: " prefix so audit trails remain attributable; plugin-supplied content is the body, not the whole message.

## Tests

- Default Commit policy reproduces today's per-op commit + message exactly (regression guard).
- Suppress on a best-effort participant: state applied, no commit written; next participant's Commit picks it up.
- Suppress on a required participant: apply errors before any state lands.
- Batch coalescing: three participants subscribed to one event, all returning Batch, produce one commit referencing all three.
- Custom message from a native plugin appears in the commit body with the safety prefix on the title.

## Coordinates with bl-26de

The SPEC needs a section on CommitPolicy. File a note on bl-26de pointing here, or fold this section into the SPEC scope when it's claimed.
title: Plugin-controlled commit policy on negotiation outcome
